title: Holding stocks is harder than finding them: 10 mid- and small-caps stocks that may be worth holding on to
summary: Holding a stock is not an act of blind faith. It is not refusing to change one’s mind. It is not averaging down because the stock has fallen. Holding is an active decision. It means asking whether the reason you bought the stock is still valid. Has the price fallen because the market is nervous, or because the company’s economics have weakened? Has the macro shock permanently damaged the business, or only delayed the earnings cycle? Is the company still sitting in a sector where demand, regulation, market structure, or execution capability gives it an edge?

title: Grading Powell’s Fed: good for stocks, bad for affordability
summary: One result of the massive monetary expansion was an upsurge in inflation, to a peak of over 9% in 2022—which the Fed infamously termed “transitory.”

title: This tech job grew 729% in a year amid AI-related layoffs. Salaries now touch  ₹1.7 crore
summary: Forward-deployed engineer job postings surged 729% between April 2025 and April 2026, with salaries topping $200,000. Anthropic, OpenAI, Palantir and McKinsey are all hiring. Here is what the role involves and why demand is exploding.

title: NSE's Electronic Gold Receipts: What are EGRs and what is in them for gold buyers?
summary: EGRs give people the option to convert their physical gold into electronic format, hold it and enjoy the upside in gold prices, and later sell it or convert back to physical gold. The gold’s purity is assured and can be bought in small quantities of 100 milligrams.

title: Nasdaq’s round-the-clock trading dream sparks debate over when markets ‘close’: What SEC suggests
summary: The SEC has approved Nasdaq's proposal to expand trading hours for NMS stocks and ETPs to 23 hours, starting December 6. This change aims to meet global trading demands and will introduce a Night Session, allowing trading between 9 PM ET and 4 AM ET.

title: Tata Steel Q4 net more than doubles on India strength, Europe recovery
summary: Tata Steel reported a sharp rise in March quarter earnings, with net profit more than doubling on strong India performance and improving European operations. Annual profit also surged. However, the company flagged rising cost pressures from West Asia tensions and ongoing regulatory challenges in Europe impacting its overseas business.

title: FPIs pull out Rs 27,000 cr in May; 2026 outflows hit Rs 2.2 lakh cr-mark

title: Why patience, value and mean reversion could define the next market cycle
summary: As global markets grapple with high valuations, geopolitical risks and AI-driven exuberance, investors are revisiting time-tested strategies like value investing and mean reversion. Tobias Carlisle’s philosophy highlights the importance of patience, discipline and contrarian thinking, especially as the gap between expensive and undervalued sectors widens across global markets.

title: India missed out on AI, and now its run as market darling may be over
summary: India's stock market faces a significant downturn as global investment shifts towards AI. Foreign investors are leaving, impacting market value.  The country's IT sector, a major driver of past success, is now vulnerable to AI automation.  This presents a critical juncture for India's economic future and its position in global markets.

title: PFC board clears next step for REC merger, seeks govt approval

The merger will be based on a share swap ratio determined by valuers, while ensuring the merged entity retains its government-company status.
